currentRuntimeBoundary.meaning: existing action-only advisory guardrail remains separate and unchanged

candidateSources.agency: California State Water Resources Control Board and local health agencies
candidateSources.meaning: Official warnings, closures, and rain advisories caused by bacterial contamination and issued for water-contact health protection.
candidateSources.spatialSupport: Only the exact agency-published station, beach, or county action geography may be used.
candidateSources.temporalSupport: Only an active action with reviewed start and end semantics is current; absence is unknown.
candidateSources.qualityBoundary: The issuing health agency remains authoritative, and an absent action is never proof of clean water.
allowedUses: suppress an exactly mapped recommendation while an official action is current

candidateSources.agency: California State Water Resources Control Board and local monitoring agencies
candidateSources.meaning: Station-level fecal indicator bacteria laboratory results collected to evaluate recreational water-contact standards.
candidateSources.spatialSupport: A result describes its sampled station and cannot be inherited by a different fishing location without reviewed support.
candidateSources.temporalSupport: A result describes its sample time; it is not a continuous present condition and cannot fill unsampled periods.
candidateSources.qualityBoundary: Method, qualifier, units, laboratory, project quality plan, completeness, and applicable contact standard require review.
allowedUses: offline source feasibility and measurement-contract research

candidateSources.agency: California Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment
candidateSources.meaning: Health-based guidance for how often defined populations may eat specified species from a water body or statewide advisory geography.
candidateSources.spatialSupport: Use only the published water-body or statewide advisory extent and its explicit species coverage.
candidateSources.temporalSupport: The current published advisory supersedes product interpretation; publication age is not a measured present concentration.
candidateSources.qualityBoundary: Population group, species, serving alternatives, contaminant basis, and statewide fallback rules must remain intact.
allowedUses: link users to current agency safe-eating guidance without paraphrasing serving arithmetic
prohibitedUses: water-contact safety or site cleanliness inference

candidateSources.agency: California Surface Water Ambient Monitoring Program Safe to Eat Workgroup
candidateSources.meaning: Fish and shellfish tissue monitoring used to assess contaminant bioaccumulation and support consumption-advisory science.
candidateSources.spatialSupport: Each tissue result is bound to its sampled water body, species, tissue preparation, and sampling design.
candidateSources.temporalSupport: A tissue observation represents its collection period and cannot be treated as a live site condition.
candidateSources.qualityBoundary: Species, size, tissue type, analyte, units, detection limits, composites, QA review, and advisory interpretation all matter.
allowedUses: offline source and species-coverage feasibility research under agency advisory authority
prohibitedUses: direct safe-to-eat recommendation that overrides OEHHA

candidateSources.meaning: Ambient monitoring results from multiple projects whose analytes, matrices, methods, qualifiers, units, and quality objectives vary.
candidateSources.spatialSupport: Each result remains bound to its station, matrix, sample depth, method, and project sampling design.
candidateSources.temporalSupport: Each result represents its collection time and does not establish conditions between observations.
candidateSources.qualityBoundary: CEDEN accepts varying quality; intended-use fitness requires project QAPP, qualifier, batch, replicate, and detection-limit review.
allowedUses: offline analyte availability and quality-metadata feasibility research
prohibitedUses: cross-project comparison before units methods and QA are harmonized

candidateSources.agency: California Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment
candidateSources.meaning: A census-tract screening tool for cumulative community pollution burden and population vulnerability in environmental-justice prioritization.
candidateSources.spatialSupport: Its census-tract community geography is not a coastal fishing-site measurement footprint.
candidateSources.temporalSupport: Its compiled indicator vintages are not live marine conditions at a trip time.
candidateSources.qualityBoundary: The composite ranking includes exposure, environmental, socioeconomic, and health indicators and cannot be relabeled as site pollution.
allowedUses: environmental-justice context research outside the fishing-site score

activationGates: freeze a fishing-quality prediction target distinct from every human-health advisory meaning
state a plausible analyte-to-species fishing mechanism before selecting any direction or weight
review source terms licensing machine interfaces and long-term access before collection
freeze exact spatial support temporal support freshness units methods qualifiers and missingness
preserve source-specific quality metadata and reject incomparable or unreviewed project results
pre-register a baseline and held-out validation protocol before inspecting candidate performance
demonstrate incremental ranking value with uncertainty and no leakage on representative held-out trips
test stale missing conflicting extreme and source-outage behavior with no automatic neutral assumption
obtain fisheries or marine ecology methods review and public-health risk-communication review
ship behind a separately versioned disabled-by-default component with rollback and drift monitoring
keep official contact and consumption advice visible authoritative and outside score interpretation
